join_order.note: Both tiny; load as flat arrays in O(1) lookup structures before main pipeline

join_order.build: part(2M rows, filter p_name LIKE '%green%') → bitset[2M bits=250KB] of ~108K qualifying partkeys
join_order.intermediate_size: ~3.28M lineitem rows pass (5.46% selectivity, empirically measured)

join_order.build: partsupp_keys_hash (pre-built mmap index, zero build cost) keyed on (ps_partkey<<32|ps_suppkey)
join_order.intermediate_size: ~3.28M rows (FK constraint: 100% match for qualifying lineitem rows)
join_order.note: Use indexes/partsupp_keys_hash.bin via mmap — avoids building 8M-row hash table at runtime

join_order.build: orders_orderkey_hash (pre-built mmap index, zero build cost)
join_order.probe: qualifying lineitem rows: l_orderkey → o_row_idx → o_orderdate → YEAR_TABLE[o_orderdate]
join_order.note: Use indexes/orders_orderkey_hash.bin via mmap — avoids building 15M-entry hash table at runtime

join_order.probe: qualifying lineitem rows: l_suppkey → nationkey → n_name
join_order.note: O(1) direct array access; suppkeys are 1..100000 in TPC-H

aggregation_detail.structure: double agg[25][8] — indexed by [nation_idx][year-1992]; nation_idx from nationkey (0-based)
aggregation_detail.rationale: 25 nations × 7 years = 175 groups; fits in 1400 bytes, stays in L1 cache, no hash needed

filter_pushdown.output_structure: bitset<2000001> — 250KB, fits in L3 cache for O(1) l_partkey membership test

data_structures.part_filter: bitset(2000001) — 250KB, O(1) membership test for green partkeys
data_structures.partsupp_join: mmap pre-built composite hash (indexes/partsupp_keys_hash.bin): int64_t key=(ps_partkey<<32|ps_suppkey), 16M slots × 16B = 268MB, zero build cost
data_structures.orders_join: mmap pre-built hash (indexes/orders_orderkey_hash.bin): int32_t key=o_orderkey, 33.5M slots × 12B = 402MB, zero build cost
data_structures.aggregation: thread_local double[25][8] flat array — 1400B per thread, fits in L1 cache; merge after parallel phase

parallelism.comment: L3=44MB / 64 threads / ~5 active columns = ~140K rows per morsel; use 64K for safe L3 locality
parallelism.lineitem_split: partition lineitem rows into 64K-row morsels; each thread processes morsels from a work queue
parallelism.synchronization: thread_local_merge — each thread accumulates into its own double[25][8]; main thread sums 64 arrays after parallel phase (trivial: 175 × 64 = 11200 additions)
parallelism.build_phases_sequential: Part bitset build (2M rows), nation/supplier arrays (tiny) — sequential before parallel probe

index_usage.layout: uint32_t ht_size=16777216; uint32_t num_positions=8000000; then ht_size × {int64_t key, uint32_t offset, uint32_t count} (16B/slot); then num_positions × uint32_t row_idx
index_usage.hash_function: uint64_t h = key * 0x9E3779B97F4A7C15ULL; h ^= h >> 33; return h & mask
index_usage.purpose: Zero-cost lookup of ps_supplycost row index for qualifying lineitem rows; avoids 8M-row hash table build

index_usage.layout: uint32_t ht_size=33554432; uint32_t num_positions=15000000; then ht_size × {int32_t key, uint32_t offset, uint32_t count} (12B/slot); then num_positions × uint32_t row_idx
index_usage.purpose: Zero-cost lookup of o_orderdate row index from l_orderkey; avoids 15M-entry hash table build

physical_plan.scan_detail: Fused loop over lineitem: (1) check l_partkey in green_bitset, (2) probe partsupp hash, (3) probe orders hash, (4) direct array lookup for suppkey→nationkey, (5) accumulate into agg[nation_idx][year-1992]

memory_detail.lineitem_scan: sequential — 5 columns × 59,986,052 × 8B = ~2.4GB, streamed sequentially; good HDD throughput
memory_detail.partsupp_hash_probes: random access into 268MB mmap region; ~3.28M probes after green filter (hot: fits in RAM, cold: OS fetches on demand)
memory_detail.orders_hash_probes: random access into 402MB mmap region; ~3.28M probes (same pattern as partsupp)
memory_detail.ps_supplycost_access: random access into partsupp/ps_supplycost.bin for ~3.28M qualifying rows; late materialization (only for rows passing green filter)

column_load_order: part/p_name.bin + part/p_partkey.bin (2M rows, 120MB — filter first to build green bitset)
indexes/partsupp_keys_hash.bin (268MB mmap — zero build, OS fetches on probe)
indexes/orders_orderkey_hash.bin (402MB mmap — zero build, OS fetches on probe)
lineitem/l_partkey.bin (480MB — filter column, load first of lineitem group)
partsupp/ps_supplycost.bin (late materialized — random access only for ~3.28M qualifying rows)
orders/o_orderdate.bin (late materialized — random access only for ~3.28M qualifying rows)
io_strategy.prefetch_detail: Issue madvise(MADV_WILLNEED) concurrently for all lineitem columns using omp parallel for; kernel dispatches parallel HDD reads
io_strategy.late_materialization: ps_supplycost.bin and o_orderdate.bin accessed only for rows passing green filter (5.46% of 60M); reduces random I/O by 18.3x vs full scan

output.sort: std::sort on 175-element result vector by (nation ASC, o_year DESC)
output.note: 175 elements — trivial cost, negligible vs scan time

breakdown.lineitem_parallel_scan: ~150ms hot (60M rows × 5 cols streamed, 64 threads, ~3.28M hash probes into mmap indexes)

sampling_results.note: FK guarantee: every qualifying (l_partkey, l_suppkey) pair has exactly one match in partsupp
